textRuns.value: Daily and Hourly don't differ by accident: each metric behaves the same way at almost every site, and has done so across all sixteen months. That consistency is the real result — the Locomizer feed is stable, and the Daily (HOUR 25) figures hold up as the reporting base.

The split is clean. Signals and their extrapolated versions add up exactly — Hourly equals Daily to the decimal at every site. Anything that counts people (Users, PaS, Total Population) runs above Daily once you sum the hours, because a visitor who stays three hours gets counted three times; on the larger sites that overcount settles around 1.5×. The reach and eye-contact metrics land below Daily (~0.7), not because audience falls but because each hour is extrapolated to its own population and the hours don't re-add to the day.

Two things worth flagging. The overcount isn't flat — it climbs with how busy a site is, from about 1.27× across the quietest quarter of the network to 1.63× across the busiest. Busier, stickier locations simply hold people over more hours. And the handful of sites showing 3× or 5× inflation are all small-panel locations where a tiny denominator swings the ratio; the large sites stay in a tight 1.4–2.0 band. Treat those spikes as noise, not signal.
For anyone using the numbers: report from Daily, keep Hourly for validation, and read inflation on the person metrics as repeat presence — a high value is normal for a busy screen, not an error.
